topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on student loans, tuition payments, and transparency in higher education financing.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend the 3.4% interest rate on Direct Stafford loans
Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to repeal provisions establishing and appropriating funds to the Prevention and Public Health Fund.
Document 2: This bill amends the federal bankruptcy code, with respect to transfers of interests that may be avoided by a bankruptcy trustee, to except tuition payments by a parent to an institution of higher education for the education of that parent's child.
Document 3: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand disclosure requirements for an institution of higher education.

topic.name: Voting Accessibility and Integrity
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving the accessibility, integrity, and security of the voting process in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Revised Statutes with respect to the time of election to establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November
Declares the sense of Congress that state and local election officials should work together to develop plans for effective transition to weekend voting.
Document 2: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 with respect to ballot verification and mandatory paper record audit capacity
Requires the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to study, test, and develop best practices to enhance accessibility of ballot verification mechanisms.
Document 3: Amends the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 to increase the length of imprisonment for criminal activities involving intimidation or fraud
Prohibit an individual involved in such activities from voting in subsequent federal elections.

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Climate Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and strategies aimed at promoting renewable energy sources, reducing greenhouse gas emissions, and enhancing energy efficiency in the United States.

Document 1: U.S. agricultural, forest, and working land should provide from renewable resources at least 25% of the total energy consumed in the United States
establish grants for state, tribal, and local governments to implement an infrastructure corridors program for renewable fuels.
Document 2: require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
specified quantities of GHG emission allowances, which decline for each of 2012 to 2050.
Document 3: direct the Department of Energy (DOE) to establish guidance for technical interconnection standards that ensure interoperability
encourage the use of distributed generation (such as combined heat and power technology and waste heat to power technology)

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Fraud Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing and addressing fraud and improper payments within Medicare and Medicaid programs.

Document 1: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act (SSA) with respect to the authority of the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to exclude from federal health programs certain individuals
Establishes criminal penalties for anyone who knowingly and with intent to defraud purchases, sells, or distributes...
Document 2: Requires states to pay dispensing fees for Medicaid covered outpatient drugs
Directs the Secretary... to collaborate with the Commissioner of Social Security for use of the Commissioner's system for obtaining information held by financial institutions
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to prohibit sponsors of prescription drug plans (PDPs) from paying claims for prescription drugs that do not include the valid National Provider Identifier
Requires the Secretary's annual report to Congress on the use of recovery audit contractors under the Medicare Integrity Program

topic.name: Drug-Related Crime Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing drug trafficking, gang violence, and crime in Indian country through increased penalties and law enforcement measures.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to increase prison terms for manufacturing, distributing, or possessing large amounts of drugs.
Directs the Attorney General to develop a National Drug Trafficking Enforcement Strategy.
Document 2: Establishes an Office of Indian Country Crime to address crime in Indian country.
Provides for tracking crimes committed in Indian country and grants to improve tribal data collection systems.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to define 'criminal street gang' and increase penalties for gang crimes.
Authorizes the Attorney General to designate areas as high intensity interstate gang activity areas.

topic.name: U.S. Economic Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting U.S. economic interests and security from foreign threats, particularly through cyber espionage and foreign investments.

Document 1: U.S. policy to deny financial resources to Cuba
deterrent measures against foreign investments enhancing Cuban regime
Document 2: cyber economic espionage should be a priority issue
increased efforts to bring economic espionage criminal cases against foreign actors
Document 3: requires the President to report on foreign countries engaging in economic espionage
authorizes blocking transactions related to economic espionage

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Economic Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to tariff suspensions, economic priorities, and technical corrections in federal laws.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing...
suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing (R)-2-Y4-(6-chloro-1,3-benzoxazol-2-yloxy)phenoxy?propionate...
Document 2: State Temporary Economic Priority (STEP) Act - Authorizes states to transfer or consolidate funds...
economic growth at an annual rate of less than 1% for two consecutive calendar quarters;
Document 3: Fix the Footnotes Act of 2015
amends the federal criminal code to make technical corrections, including grammar, and other corrections.

topic.name: Aviation Security and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the security and operational efficiency of air transportation systems in the United States.

Document 1: airport checkpoint wait times should not take priority over the security of the nation's aviation system
TSA shall begin and complete an assessment of its staffing allocation model
Document 2: impose a fee on an air carrier that charges a fee for the first bag checked
fees to be credited as offsetting collections to the Secretary for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA)

topic.name: Environmental Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting conservation and sustainable management of natural resources.

Document 1: Coral Reef Conservation Amendments Act of 2007
direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Document 2: Senator Paul Simon Water for the World Act of 2014
the United States should be a global leader in helping provide sustainable access to clean water and sanitation for the world's most vulnerable populations.
Document 3: Rural Opportunities Act of 2007
make state grants for extension, education, applied research, and development activities at institutions of higher education.

topic.name: Tax Legislation Changes
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at modifying existing tax laws and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits officers or employees of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) from initiating any new audit of any taxpayer for 180 days after enactment of this Act.
HALT the IRS Act
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal a provision that extends to corporations that are not tax-exempt the requirement to report payments of $600 or more.
Small Business Paperwork Mandate Elimination Act
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the alternative minimum tax (AMT) on individuals after 2010.
Individual AMT Repeal Act of 2011

topic.name: Tariff Amendments and Trade Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to tariff laws affecting various goods and trade agreements.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930
apply the Act's antidumping and countervailing duty provisions
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain untwisted filament yarns
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
extend the temporary suspension of duty on yarn of carded cashmere

topic.name: Environmental and Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation aimed at protecting natural resources and enhancing infrastructure through environmental regulations and project authorizations.

Document 1: Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery protection
conduct a benchmark stock assessment
Document 2: authorize and assist in the construction of a new bridge
specified mitigation items described in the 2006 St. Croix River Crossing Project Memorandum of Understanding
Document 3: amends the Safe Drinking Water Act
require the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate a national primary drinking water regulation

topic.name: Tax and Social Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to tax incentives, Social Security benefits, and Medicare enrollment for individuals and families.

Document 1: increases and expands tax incentives for employer-provided dependent care assistance
establishes tax credits for small employer dependent care assistance program start-up costs
Document 2: amends title II of the Social Security Act to increase the contribution and benefit base
formulates a minimum monthly insurance benefit
Document 3: establish a 24-month extended special enrollment period for Medicare part B
requires individuals enrolled in part B during the extended special enrollment period to pay a monthly premium increased by 25%

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Charitable Contributions and Education
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code that provide tax incentives for charitable contributions, educational expenses, and research activities.

Document 1: modify the tax deduction for charitable contributions of food inventory
make such deduction permanent
Document 2: allow a tax deduction, up to $10,000 per year, for supplemental education expenses
expenses not required for the a student's enrollment or attendance
Document 3: increase the rate of the tax credit for increasing research activities
extend such tax credit through 2012

topic.name: Tribal Land Management and Trusts
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, trust status, and use of land by Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Directs that approximately 26,565 acres of land in Nevada... be held in trust for the benefit of the Moapa Band of Paiutes.
Requires the Tribe to use the land for traditional and customary uses, stewardship conservation...
Document 2: Reaffirms the trust status of land taken into trust by the United States pursuant to the Indian Reorganization Act...
Makes this Act applicable to all claims pending on, or filed on or after, the date of enactment of this Act.
Document 3: Extends to any Indian tribe the discretion... to lease restricted lands without the approval of the Secretary of the Interior.

topic.name: Government Policy Initiatives
topic.description: Documents related to various government policy initiatives and directives in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to prepare an interagency report on climate change and energy policy in China and India
submit such report to specified committees of Congress.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Air Force to construct additions and alterations to the flight simulator facility
using amounts authorized to be appropriated for FY2009 under this Act.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to carry out, and report to Congress on, a study on the acquisition of a specified parcel of land
to be used for expansion of the Beaufort National Cemetery.

topic.name: Insurance Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at regulating the insurance industry while ensuring consumer protection and preventing discrimination.

Document 1: Prohibit a consumer reporting agency from furnishing consumer reports for use in making decisions that result in racial or ethnic discrimination
Excludes insurance information derived from specified databases.
Document 2: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to establish the National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers
Retains states' regulatory authority over licensing and consumer protection.
Document 3: Defines 'captive insurance company' and its exclusions from nonadmitted insurer requirements
Clarifies the primary purpose of captive insurance companies.

topic.name: Election and Public Policy Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating electoral contributions, public funds in labor relations, and transparency in census-related mailings.

Document 1: ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals
domestic corporations in which one or more foreign principals have an ownership interest
Document 2: limitations on the use of public funds or property
assist, deter, or promote union organizing
Document 3: mailing soliciting the purchase of a product or service
accurate name and return address of the entity sending the mailing

topic.name: Legislative Updates and Proposals
topic.description: This cluster covers various legislative updates and proposals related to military infrastructure, shipping regulations, and law enforcement funding.

Document 1: updates and improvements to reflect amendments made to newly codified title 46
Shipping of the United States Code
Document 2: directs the Department of the Army to submit to Congress a report
plans to extend the runway at Pope Army Airfield
Document 3: amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968
waive the grantee matching funds requirement for purchasing bulletproof armor vests

topic.name: Cybersecurity and Transportation Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and measures aimed at enhancing security protocols in cybersecurity and transportation systems, particularly focusing on threat information sharing and access control regulations.

Document 1: Authorizes private entities to employ countermeasures and use cybersecurity systems to obtain, identify, or possess cyber threat information
Permits contractors to also provide such information to a cybersecurity center.
Document 2: The TSA shall update DHS rules on access controls, considering specified matters.
The TSA shall issue guidance to airport operators for placing an expiration date on each airport credential.

topic.name: Immigration and Employment Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts to reform immigration policies and employment practices in the United States.

Document 1: establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants
increases immigration visas for unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens
Document 2: prohibits the Secretary of Labor from issuing any special wage certificate
allows them to pay individuals with disabilities subminimum wages
Document 3: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa
whose commercial activities will generate required levels of employment, revenue, or capital investment

topic.name: Government Accountability and Redistricting
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the appointment of government officials and the establishment of independent bodies for fair redistricting processes.

Document 1: Requires the Governor of the Virgin Islands to appoint a Chief Financial Officer
Establishes the Virgin Islands Chief Financial Officer Search Commission
Document 2: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment
Requires such redistricting to be conducted through a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission
Document 3: Requires each state to establish an independent redistricting commission
Prohibits consideration, in developing a redistricting plan, of the political party affiliation of the population of a district

topic.name: Alternative Fuels and Tax Incentives
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting alternative fuels and regulating oil industry tax benefits in the United States.

Document 1: allow an excise tax credit through 2027 for alternative fuels and fuel mixtures involving compressed or liquefied natural gas
Requires 50% of all new vehicles purchased or placed in service by the U.S. government by December 31, 2014, to be capable of operating on compressed or liquefied natural gas
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers who drive a highway vehicle fueled by gasoline or diesel fuel a $1,000 tax credit in 2008
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to deny to taxpayers with gross revenues in excess of $100 million in a taxable year the tax deduction for intangible drilling and development costs
Imposes on producers of taxable crude oil or natural gas a 13% excise tax on the removal price of such oil and natural gas produced from lands on the Outer Continental Shelf in the Gulf of Mexico

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and resources for small businesses through technology, regulatory assistance, and mentorship programs.

Document 1: direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to assign an SBA employee to coordinate SBA programs and activities relating to broadband and emerging information technology
requires reports from the coordinator to the congressional small business committees on coordinator activities
Document 2: establish a program to provide regulatory compliance assistance to small businesses
confidential counseling to owners and operators of small businesses regarding compliance with federal and state regulations
Document 3: authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a mentor-protege program for all small businesses
assist proteges to compete for federal prime contracts and subcontracts

topic.name: Data Protection and Fraud Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting personal information and preventing fraud against vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Prohibits data brokers from obtaining or causing to be disclosed personal information
Requires data brokers to provide individuals a cost-free means to review their personal or identifying information.
Document 2: Expands the reporting requirements of electronic communication and remote computing service providers
Imposes civil penalties on service providers who fail to report suspected child exploitation or pornography.
Document 3: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to establish an office within the Bureau of Consumer Protection to advise the FTC on the prevention of fraud targeting seniors
Disseminate to seniors and their families and caregivers information on the most common fraud schemes.

topic.name: Small Business Procurement Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing opportunities and protections for small businesses in federal contracting processes.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to consolidate Small Business Administration (SBA) provisions relating to contract bundling
Requires the head of an agency to conduct market research to determine whether bundling is necessary and justified.
Document 2: Increases the government-wide small business procurement contract goal
Requires the total value of all prime contracts for a fiscal year to include the value of each contract awarded.
Document 3: Requires each federal agency to establish a plan for the periodic review of its rules that have a significant economic impact on a substantial number of small entities
Each covered rule shall cease to have effect seven years after the final version of such rule is published unless renewed by the agency.

topic.name: Veterans Mental Health Services
topic.description: Focuses on the provision and evaluation of mental health services for veterans and active military members, particularly related to PTSD and women's health.

Document 1: outreach and training services related to the mental health of veterans
counseling centers, student health or wellness centers
Document 2: in-person mental health screening for each member of the Armed Forces
identify post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), suicidal tendencies, and other behavioral health issues
Document 3: comprehensive review of women-specific health services
report review results to the congressional defense committees

topic.name: Advanced Computing and Technology Innovation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at promoting advanced computing technologies and innovations to enhance scientific research, telecommunications, and nanotechnology applications in the United States.

Document 1: Defines 'exascale computing' as computing through the use of a computing machine that performs near or above 10 to the 18th power floating point operations per second.
Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct a research program to develop exascale computing machine architectures.
Document 2: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) and the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information to develop a plan to increase sharing of spectrum.
Requires the director of the National Science Foundation (NSF) to establish a program of basic research in advanced information and communications technologies.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a program to award prizes to eligible persons for achievement in one or more applications of nanotechnology.
Allows the board to make awards under the program by holding a financial award competition.

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the management, designation, and conveyance of federal lands, particularly in relation to wilderness areas and national forests.

Document 1: Expands the boundaries of the Cibola National Forest
Requires the Manzano Wilderness Study Area to be designated as wilderness
Document 2: Releases from further study for wilderness designation
is no longer subject to the land use restrictions of the Roadless Area Conservation Rule
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey, without consideration, to the city of Anchorage
to enable the economic development of such land

topic.name: Water Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving water management, security, and infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: requires the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to provide grants to establish and maintain centers of excellence for innovative stormwater control infrastructure
promote sharing and integration of the infrastructure within the region
Document 2: amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to revise and expand requirements for assessment by covered water systems
requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate regulations to establish risk-based performance standards for the security of such systems
Document 3: establishes the Water Protection and Reinvestment Trust Fund to support investments in clean water infrastructure
requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to allocate amounts from the Trust Fund for specified clean water programs

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Extensions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments that extend deadlines or modify existing provisions in federal laws.

Document 1: Amends the Antitrust Modernization Commission Act of 2002 to extend the termination date
extend the termination date of the Antitrust Modernization Commission to 60 days
Document 2: Amends the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act to eliminate the 15-year limitation
borrowers of agricultural operating loans are eligible for guaranteed assistance
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal a provision
requirement to report payments of $600 or more

topic.name: Coast Guard and Transportation Security
topic.description: Legislation focused on the authorization and funding of the Coast Guard and Transportation Security Administration operations.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2011 appropriations for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies, materials, and human factors relating to the Coast Guard's mission
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Homeland Security for the Transportation Security Administration
Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security to report on TSA use of a risk-based system for allocating security resources effectively
Document 3: This bill authorizes appropriations for the Coast Guard for FY2016-FY2017
additional appropriations are authorized for FY2016-FY2017 for preacquisition activities for a new polar icebreaker

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting various tax deductions and credits.

Document 1: extend the new markets tax credit through 2014
allow an offset against the alternative minimum tax (AMT) for such credit
Document 2: repeal the restrictions on the tax deduction for the travel expenses of a taxpayer's spouse
tax deduction for business travel
Document 3: allow taxpayers who do not otherwise itemize a deduction from gross income
amounts paid to provide care packages for members of the Armed Forces

topic.name: Abortion and Health Coverage Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the funding and coverage of abortion services in health care plans.

Document 1: requires health plans to cover breast cancer treatment including inpatient stays
prohibits health insurers from avoiding coverage requirements
Document 2: prohibits federal funding for abortion services
exempts abortions in cases of rape, incest, or life endangerment
Document 3: prohibits federal funds from being used for health benefits coverage that includes abortion
amends tax code to exclude plans covering abortion from tax credits

topic.name: Land Exchange and Access Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on land exchange, access easements, and land entitlement for specific groups and regions in the United States.

Document 1: This bill directs the Department of Agriculture (USDA)...
The nonexclusive perpetual access easement granted to BHI shall allow...

Grants Sealaska nonexclusive easements to certain forest development roads...
Document 3: Amends the National Trails System Act...
federal authority to acquire fee title shall be limited to an average of not more than one-quarter mile on either side of such national historic trails...

topic.name: Environmental Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative acts focused on environmental management, including wildlife control, wildfire funding, and marine resource programs.

Document 1: Amends the National Sea Grant College Program Act
Requires that sea grant colleges provide extension services
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to submit an annual report on animals killed
make such report publicly available on the Internet
Document 3: amends the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act
requires specified adjustments to discretionary spending limits for wildfire suppression operations

topic.name: Federal Innovation Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at enhancing federal support for innovation and research through programs like SBIR, STTR, and competitive prize initiatives.

Document 1: reauthorize through: (1) FY2022 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR); and (2) FY2023 the Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
increases by 0.1% per year, from FY2010-FY2019, the percentage of participating federal agencies' extramural research budget to be set aside for SBIR program activities.
Document 2: amends the Education Sciences Reform Act of 2002 (ESRA) to require the Department of Education (ED), in collecting information and data under that Act, to avoid duplicating reporting requirements
The mission of the Institute of Education Sciences (IES) must include providing national leadership in expanding fundamental knowledge and understanding of adult education.
Document 3: authorize each head of a federal agency to carry out a program to award prizes competitively to stimulate innovation
requires the agency head to widely advertise each competition to encourage broad participation.

topic.name: Tax Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to tax reforms, credits, and provisions affecting individuals and small businesses.

Document 1: Makes permanent the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 for individual taxpayers
Increases the capital gains tax rate from 15% to 20% for taxpayers whose taxable income exceeds the $400,000 threshold amount.
Document 2: The bill modifies tax provisions, regulatory requirements, and legal procedures that affect small businesses
Temporarily reduces the capital gains tax on certain small business stock.
Document 3: Allows owners of eligible dwelling units a new tax credit for up to 50% of the lead hazard reduction activity costs
Limits the amount of the credit in any taxable year to $3,000 for specified abatement measures.

topic.name: National Security Surveillance Reform
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at reforming the National Security Letters and the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act to enhance privacy protections and oversight.

Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that each decision, order, or opinion issued by the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court should be declassified
Amends FISA provisions concerning access to business records and the targeting of persons reasonably believed to be located outside the United States.

topic.name: Education Improvement Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at enhancing educational opportunities and outcomes for students through various programs and grants.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states that make school attendance compulsory through age 17.
Requires states to use their grant to improve their secondary school programs.
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to establish a geography education grant program.
Requires program participants to use their grants and subgrants to enhance the geographic literacy of students in kindergarten through grade 12.
Document 3: Directs the Corporation for National and Community Service to award a contract to a nonprofit organization to establish a seven-year pilot program.
Requires such coaches to build a strong college-going culture for all students in high schools with historically low college attendance rates.

topic.name: Water Reclamation and Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures and initiatives aimed at improving water reclamation, treatment, and infrastructure resilience in response to changing hydrological conditions.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in a project to reclaim, reuse, and treat groundwater and wastewater
limits the federal share of the project to 25%
Document 2: requires the Environmental Protection Agency to establish the Water Infrastructure Resiliency and Sustainability Program
provide grants for programs or projects to increase the resiliency or adaptability of water systems
Document 3: conduct feasibility studies on projects that address water shortages
requires that such study comply with Bureau of Reclamation policy standards

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing benefits and protections for veterans and military personnel, including retirement eligibility and employment preferences.

Document 1: Amends federal law concerning receipt of retired pay for non-regular service
retirement eligibility age shall be reduced below 60
Document 2: Amends the Defense Base Act to require satisfaction through the Government Defense Base Act self-insurance program
insurance and benefits requirements imposed on defense contractors

Document 3: Includes as a preference eligible for federal employment purposes the parent of an individual who lost his or her life under honorable conditions while serving in the Armed Forces
service-connected permanently and totally disabled veteran.

topic.name: Retirement and Education Financing Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at reforming retirement plans and financing education through innovative agreements.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to declare that an organization otherwise eligible to participate in a church plan shall not be aggregated with another such organization
Allows church plans and their supporting organizations to invest plan assets in a group trust
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code (IRC) to repeal the 10% cap on the qualified percentage of an employee's compensation
Requires separate application of the rules for a top-heavy defined benefit plan to any defined contribution plan covering part-time employees
Document 3: Declares that income share agreements that meet this Act's requirements are valid, binding, and enforceable contracts
Defines an 'income share agreement' as an agreement between an individual and any other person under which the individual commits to pay a specified percentage of the individual's future income

topic.name: Post Office Designations
topic.description: The documents discuss the formal designations of various United States Postal Service facilities and federal buildings in honor of individuals.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 6310 North University Street in Peoria, Illinois, as the 'Charles 'Chip' Lawrence Chan Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of General Services to ensure that the federally occupied building located at McKinley Avenue and Third Street, S.W., Canton, Ohio, is known and designated as the 'Ralph Regula Federal Building and United States Courthouse.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 787 State Route 17M in Monroe, New York, as the 'National Clandestine Service of the Central Intelligence Agency NCS Officer Gregg David Wenzel Memorial Post Office.'

topic.name: Trade Adjustment Assistance Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at providing support and assistance to workers adversely affected by trade and globalization, particularly through trade adjustment assistance programs.

Document 1: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to allow the filing of a petition for certification of eligibility to apply for trade adjustment assistance by adversely affected workers in a service sector firm.
Requires the Secretary of Labor to conduct a study on ways to improve the timeliness and coverage of data on trade in services.
Document 2: Amends the Trade and Globalization Adjustment Assistance Act of 2009 to extend trade adjustment assistance programs through February 12, 2011.
Authorizes appropriations for the reemployment TAA program through February 12, 2011.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt employers from social security and railroad retirement taxes for new employees hired after February 3, 2010, to incentivize hiring.
Appropriates amounts necessary to cover any reduction in revenues resulting from the tax exemptions.

topic.name: Legal Rights and Protections
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legal rights and protections related to personal choices, public safety, and environmental accountability.

Document 1: fundamental right to choose to: (1) bear a child; (2) terminate a pregnancy prior to fetal viability;
prohibits a federal, state, or local governmental entity from denying or interfering with a woman's right
Document 2: Bars the promulgation or enforcement of any regulation that prohibits an individual from possessing a firearm
the possession of the firearm is in compliance with the law of the state in which the unit is located
Document 3: require all claims against each responsible party for removal costs or damages under such Act to be presented first to the responsible party
provide that nothing in such Act shall affect or preempt the authority of any state from imposing on any responsible party

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Regulations

it shall not be unlawful under the antitrust laws for the National Football League to enforce rules

permit recreational fishing for Atlantic Striped Bass in the Block Island Sound transit zone
Document 3: Children's Sleepwear Labeling Act of 2011
require flame resistant children's sleepwear to include a label

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing services and protections for vulnerable populations, including homeless youth, veterans, and victims of violence.

serve youth in a manner that is culturally competent.
Document 2: to develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts
for programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems who have served in the U.S. military.
Document 3: to award grants to strengthen the response of state, tribal, territorial, or local health care systems to domestic violence
training to health care professionals on domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault and stalking

topic.name: Tariff Suspension for Manufacturing Goods
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States focusing on the temporary suspension of duties for various manufacturing-related products.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on certain manufacturing equipment.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain protective cases of molded silicone for toys.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain work boots for women.

topic.name: Healthcare Education and Training
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving education and training for healthcare professionals in various fields, including palliative care, mental health, and medical residency programs.

Document 1: require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants or contracts for Palliative Care and Hospice Education Centers
improve the training of health professionals in palliative care
Document 2: criteria for the certification of federally-qualified community behavioral health centers

Document 3: direct the Administrator of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to make grants to or contracts with eligible partnerships
