topic.name: U.S. Legislative Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments affecting U.S. laws and policies related to immigration, agriculture, and small business support.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
loss of nationality for supporting hostilities against the United States.
Document 2: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
suspend the limitation on loan guarantee assistance.
Document 3: Extends authorization for programs under the Small Business Act
currently authorized through July 31, 2009.

topic.name: Food Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring the safety and regulation of food products and related public health measures.

Document 1: require a person... to notify the Secretary of Agriculture or the Secretary of Health and Human Services
provide the Secretary of Agriculture with authority for voluntary and mandatory nondistribution and recall
Document 2: direct the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to publish a health advisory for perchlorate
require consumer confidence reports by community water systems to disclose the presence of perchlorate in drinking water
Document 3: establish the Food Safety Administration (FSA) as an independent agency to administer and enforce food safety laws
promulgate regulations to protect the food supply from contamination

topic.name: Tribal and State Sovereignty
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legal frameworks and rights pertaining to the sovereignty of tribal nations and states in relation to federal authority.

Document 1: Allows the Coquille Indian Tribe to transfer, lease, encumber, or otherwise convey real property without further authorization from the United States.
Prohibits the Tribe from transferring land held in trust by the United States.
Document 2: Prohibits federal funds from being used to limit or control the type of firearm ammunition or fishing tackle on federal lands.
Allows exceptions based on scientific evidence regarding detrimental effects on local wildlife.
Document 3: Reaffirms each state's autonomous sovereign police powers under the Tenth Amendment.
Authorizes state officials to file civil actions against federal interference with state authority.

topic.name: Early Childhood Education and Teacher Effectiveness
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments aimed at improving early childhood education standards and teacher effectiveness within the K-12 education system.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to coordinate school improvement and professional development activities with early childhood development and education programs.
Requires states to encourage local educational agencies (LEAs) and elementary schools to use school improvement funds to offer early childhood education programs from birth to school entry.
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to exempt teachers who teach multiple subjects in certain small middle or secondary schools from being required to be highly qualified in each of their subjects if they: (1) are highly qualified in at least one; and (2) demonstrate highly effective teaching skills on a state performance assessment.
Permits local educational agencies (LEAs) identified as needing improvement to provide supplemental educational services themselves.
Document 3: Requires each state receiving school improvement funds to define teacher and principal effectiveness and establish performance ratings.
Requires teacher evaluations to emphasize student academic growth.

topic.name: U.S. Military Engagement and Oversight
topic.description: Legislation focused on U.S. military operations, funding, and human rights oversight in foreign nations.

Document 1: Prohibits FY2014 Department of Defense (DOD) funds from being made available to the government of Burma unless the Secretary of State certifies to Congress that: Burma has taken steps toward establishing civilian oversight of the armed forces, addressing human rights abuses by the military.
Directs the Secretary of Defense to report annually to Congress on the status of military-to-military engagement between the U.S. Armed Forces and the Burmese military.
Document 2: Prohibits the obligation or expenditure of funds for the purpose of establishing or maintaining a permanent U.S. military presence in Iraq.
Directs the President, within 30 days after enactment of this Act, to report to Congress outlining a strategy to end U.S. combat operations in Iraq.
Document 3: Prohibits Department of Defense (DOD) funds for FY2012-FY2013 from being used to provide the Russian Federation with access to U.S. missile defense technology.

Expresses the sense of Congress that an agreement regarding missile defense cooperation between the United States and the Russian Federation shall not be legally or politically binding unless it is specifically approved with the advice and consent of the Senate.

topic.name: Family and Community Engagement in Education
topic.description: This topic focuses on initiatives and legislation aimed at enhancing family and community involvement in the education system to improve student outcomes.

Document 1: require the Secretary of Education to award grants to states for competitive subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs) to assist public elementary and secondary schools to hire and maintain Parent and Community Outreach Coordinators
to connect integrated services and comprehensive supports to students and families to enhance student performance.
Document 2: authorize states to reserve school improvement funds to award grants to nonprofit organizations that partner with local educational agencies (LEAs) or schools to establish and operate Local Family Engagement Centers
develop and implement a statewide family engagement in education plan.

topic.name: U.S. Border and National Security Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative and strategic efforts related to border security, immigration enforcement, and national defense against threats such as terrorism and public health crises.

Document 1: U.S. global strategy to defeat al Qaeda and its affiliates.
Directs the Secretaries of Defense, State, and Homeland Security.
Document 2: Assessing Progress in Haiti Act.
Status of post-earthquake recovery and development efforts in Haiti.
Document 3: Establishes the Department of Homeland Security Border Oversight Commission.
Requires the Commission to recommend border enforcement policy improvements.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at improving services and payment structures within Medicare and Medicaid programs.

Document 1: Medicare Imaging Disclosure Sunshine Act of 2008
requires physicians referring an individual for imaging to inform the individual at the time of referral
Document 2: Home Health Care Planning Improvement Act of 2010
allows payment for home health services to Medicare beneficiaries by various healthcare providers
Document 3: Nursing School Clinics Act of 2009
provides for coverage of nursing school clinic services

topic.name: Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws and regulations aimed at protecting the rights of workers in various aspects of employment, including child labor, discrimination, and sick leave.

Document 1: Child Labor Protection Act of 2007 - Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to increase civil penalties for violations of child labor requirements.
Establishes criminal penalties for child labor violations.
Document 2: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity.
Provides for enforcement, including giving the EEOC the same enforcement powers as they have under specified provisions of the Civil Rights Act of 1964.
Document 3: Requires certain employers to permit each employee to earn at least one hour of paid sick time for every 30 hours worked.
Prohibits an employer from interfering with an employee's exercise of such rights.

topic.name: Federal Legislation on Economic and Operational Policies
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal legislative measures related to economic development, postal service operations, and aviation safety regulations.

Document 1: Prohibits a state or political subdivision from exercising its power of eminent domain over property to be used for economic development.
Renders a state or political subdivision ineligible for any federal economic development funds for a period of two fiscal years.
Document 2: Modifies the formula for contributions to the Civil Service Retirement System (CSRS) and the Federal Employees Retirement System (FERS) for postal employees.
Allows USPS to request and use up to $6 billion of any funding surplus for payment of its debt obligations.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend excise taxes on aviation fuels and air transportation.
Extends project grant authority for the airport improvement program (AIP).

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at managing, conserving, and utilizing public lands in the United States for environmental, recreational, and defense-related purposes.

Document 1: Establishes the Tule Springs Fossil Beds National Monument consisting of approximately 22,650 acres of public land in Clark County, Nevada
Requires the Secretary to develop a management plan that provides for the long-term protection and management of the Monument.
Document 2: Authorizes certain land exchanges with specified landowners for the benefit of the Cathedral Rock and Horse Heaven Wildernesses
Each land exchange to be carried out in accordance with the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976.
Document 3: Withdraws 18,644 acres of public lands and interests in Broadwater County, Montana
Prohibits the Army from restricting mining on the land.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection and welfare of children, particularly in the context of immigration and child support enforcement.

Document 1: unaccompanied alien child who has not been a victim of a severe form of trafficking
placed in removal proceedings
Document 2: U.S. compliance with any multilateral child support convention
access to the Federal Parent Locator Service

topic.name: Food Security and Nutrition Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative initiatives aimed at improving access to nutritious food and enhancing food security through various programs and grants.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to carry out the Green and Healthy Corner Store Initiative to assist qualified convenience stores to expand their offering of fruits and vegetables.
Directs the Secretary to make grants to provide access to farmers' markets for communities with limited access to affordable and nutritious food.
Document 2: Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 regarding the supplemental food assistance program (SNAP) to include as a retail store an agricultural producer marketing agricultural products directly to consumers.
Increases funding for community food projects and the seniors farmers' market nutrition program.
Document 3: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 with respect to producers, including peanut producers, with 10 or fewer base acres.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to ensure that no penalty is assessed against producers for failure to submit reports or comply with other program requirements.

topic.name: Chronic Disease Management and Surveillance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts to enhance the prevention, management, and surveillance of chronic diseases through data collection and public health initiatives.

Document 1: Blueprint for Health program to provide a national infrastructure for disease prevention
promote multidisciplinary care coordination, prevent disease, improve quality
Document 2: develop a system to collect data on multiple sclerosis (MS)
establish a national surveillance system for the collection and storage of such data
Document 3: develop a multisite gestational diabetes research project
expand and intensify public health research on gestational diabetes

topic.name: Animal Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster covers various legislative measures aimed at protecting animal welfare and regulating trade practices related to animals and animal products.

Document 1: makes it illegal to possess, buy, sell, transport, or trade shark fins
Penalties are imposed for violations under the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act.
Document 2: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder'
Requires dealers to include on licensing applications and annual renewals the total number of dogs exempted from exercise on the premises of the dealer.
Document 3: Amends the Fur Products Labeling Act to eliminate the exemption to fur labeling requirements
Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to publish in the Federal Register notice of, and an opportunity to comment on, a review of the Fur Products Name Guide.

topic.name: U.S. National Security and Due Process
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and policies regarding the balance between national security, the use of force, and the protection of due process rights for U.S. citizens and foreign entities.

Document 1: Prohibits anyone, including the President, from instructing an employee or an agent of the United States from engaging in, or conspiring to engage in, the extrajudicial killing of a U.S. citizen.
Defines 'extrajudicial killing' to mean a premeditated and intentional use of lethal force against a U.S. citizen.
Document 2: Requires such trial and proceedings to have all due process provided under the Constitution.
Disposition under the law of war shall only mean transfer for trial and proceedings by a court established under article III of the Constitution.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the Donbass People's Militia has met the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization.
Prohibits the Secretary from issuing any visa to, and directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to deny U.S. entry to, any member or representative of the Donbass People's Militia.

topic.name: Federal Energy Policy and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating and managing energy production and resources on federal lands in the United States.

Document 1: direct the Secretary of the Interior to publish a Quadrennial Federal Onshore Energy Production Strategy
promote the energy and national security of the United States
Document 2: require lessees to report and pay royalties on oil and gas production each month based on the actual volume
defined as any oil and gas that is produced, sold, vented, flared, used for beneficial purposes, leaked, or otherwise emitted during production
Document 3: directs the President to review all projects for the exploration, development, or production of oil and gas resources
requires the projects to be approved and authorized to proceed upon a written finding by the President that they serve the public interest

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Equipment
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures that provide tax relief or credits related to specific types of equipment and machinery.

Document 1: reduces from 10% to 3% the excise tax rate that applies to the sale of portable, electronically-aerated bait containers
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 2: impose a vehicle mileage tax on mobile mounted concrete boom pump vehicles
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 3: allows a 30% energy tax credit for equipment used to fabricate solar energy property
amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Tax Act of 2009

topic.name: Trade and Economic Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on trade provisions, humanitarian aid, and patent regulations affecting economic activities.

Document 1: Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act
preferential trade provisions
Document 2: Humanitarian Firefighter Relief Act
duty-free treatment of donated fire-fighting and rescue and relief equipment
Document 3: not a patent infringement
repair of the article of manufacture

topic.name: Spectrum Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the management, auctioning, and regulation of electromagnetic spectrum usage and rights.

Document 1: Amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act to allow federal entities that utilize government station licenses to participate in the incentive auction program
Establishes in the Treasury a Federal Spectrum Incentive Fund to be administered by the Office of Management and Budget
Document 2: Prohibits a provider of commercial mobile communications service from receiving universal service support under specified provisions of the Communications Act of 1934
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to provide that, if consistent with the public interest for a licensee to voluntarily relinquish some or all of its spectrum usage rights
the proceeds from the use of a competitive bidding system may be shared with any licensee who agreed to voluntarily relinquish such usage rights

topic.name: Small Business Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at improving regulatory processes and reducing the economic impact of regulations on small businesses and entities.

Document 1: Grants the consent of Congress to the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement
Authorize each state that is a party to the Agreement to require all remote sellers...
Document 2: Amends the Regulatory Flexibility Act of 1980 to define the economic impact of a proposed or final rule
Requires initial and final regulatory flexibility analyses under RFA to describe alternatives to a proposed rule...
Document 3: Amends the Regulatory Flexibility Act to revise the regulatory process with respect to small entities
Defines economic impact with respect to a proposed or final rule to mean any direct economic effect of a rule on small entities...

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments and provisions related to the Higher Education Act, focusing on institutional definitions and student assistance programs.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to revise and reauthorize HEA programs.

Revises the general definition of an institution of higher education (IHE) to include schools that: (1) admit students whose secondary education was conducted in a home school setting; (2) award degrees that are acceptable for admission to a graduate or professional degree program.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes related to the temporary suspension of duties on specific chemical compounds and materials in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on N,N-hexane-1,6-diylbis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxy-phenylpropionamide))
temporary suspension of duty
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on ion exchange resin, polystyrene crosslinked with divinylbenzene, chloromethylated, trimethylammonium salt
suspend temporarily the duty on ion exchange resin
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on ion-exchange resin of Benzene, diethenyl, polymer with ethenylbenzene and ethenylethylbenzene, chloromethylated, trimethylaminoquaternized
suspend temporarily the duty on ion-exchange resin

topic.name: Controlled Substances Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating the distribution, prescribing, and penalties associated with controlled substances and related drugs.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to double the term of imprisonment and triple the fine for the prohibited distribution of a schedule II or schedule III controlled substance by the operator of a pill mill.

Defines 'pill mill' to include a doctor's office, clinic, or health care facility that routinely prescribes or dispenses controlled substances outside the scope of the prevailing standards of medical practice.
Document 2: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to exclude any drug or biological product approved for rare diseases from the annual fee on manufacturers or importers.
Makes this Act effective as if included in PPACA.
Document 3: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to expand the list of substances defined as 'anabolic steroids'.
Imposes enhanced criminal and civil penalties for possessing or trafficking in any anabolic steroid.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the benefits and memorialization of military personnel and veterans.

Document 1: Federal Submerged Lands Transportation Development Act - Authorizes the Commandant of the Coast Guard to lease tidelands and submerged lands under the control of the Coast Guard for periods longer than five years.
rental or lease monies received to be deposited in the Treasury.
Document 2: Makes eligible for interment or inurnment at Arlington National Cemetery members of the Army who served honorably for at least nine months in the Tomb Guard Platoon.
provides sentinels at the Tomb of the Unknowns at such Cemetery.
Document 3: Memorialize Our Guardsmen and Reservists Act - Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to make available for purchase a memorial headstone or marker.
for the marked or unmarked grave of an individual who performed inactive duty training or active duty for training.

topic.name: Regulatory Safety Standards
topic.description: The documents discuss various acts aimed at establishing regulatory safety standards in different sectors, including pharmaceuticals, mining, and aviation.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish requirements to facilitate the tracing of drug products through the pharmaceutical supply distribution chain.
Requires drug manufacturers to provide transaction documentation before, or at the time of, each transfer of ownership of a product.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require emergency response plans to provide for communication systems between underground and surface personnel.
Requires the Secretary of Labor to issue interim final regulations... requiring each emergency response plan to provide for the installation of portable rescue chambers.
Document 3: Requires the FAA Administrator to establish the FAA Task Force on Air Carrier Safety and Pilot Training to evaluate and report on best practices in the air carrier industry.
Directs the FAA Administrator to conduct a rulemaking proceeding to require air carriers to provide flight crewmembers with ground training and flight training.

topic.name: Veterans Housing and Loan Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving housing loan access and affordability for veterans and related housing finance mechanisms.

Document 1: Increases from 90% to 100% of the reasonable value of a veteran's dwelling or farm residence the amount of the loan to be guaranteed or made in the case of a loan to refinance certain loans.
Prohibits collecting a fee from a veteran for a housing loan, unless the loan is used to refinance indebtedness.
Document 2: Establishes the National Infrastructure Development Corporation and the National Infrastructure Insurance Corporation as wholly owned Government corporations that shall conduct their respective businesses as self-supporting entities.
Document 3: Increases the maximum original principal obligation of certain mortgages originated between July 1, 2007, and December 31, 2008, that may be purchased by the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation Association (Freddie Mac).
Expresses the sense of Congress that securitization of mortgages by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac plays an important role in providing liquidity to the U.S. housing markets.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures related to the management, development, and conservation of water resources in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Central Valley Project Improvement Act (CVPIA) to include among the Act's purposes to ensure that water dedicated to fish and wildlife purposes is replaced...
Facilitate and expedite water transfers in accordance with that Act.
Document 2: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 (WRDA 1986) to allow the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to provide technical assistance to nonfederal interests...
The Corps of Engineers may partner with nonfederal interests to maintain a project.

topic.name: Higher Education Funding Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at reforming funding mechanisms and accountability in higher education institutions.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to require the Secretary of Education to: (1) use corrected data and information to recalculate the cohort default rates for institutions of higher education (IHEs) that have engaged in default manipulation

Directs the Secretary to establish an Institutional Risk-Sharing Commission to study and make recommendations for the implementation of a new risk-sharing system that holds IHEs that participate in the DL program financially accountable for poor student outcomes.
Document 2: Direct the Secretary of Education to conduct studies regarding the feasibility of, and options for, implementing the Pay It Forward model for funding postsecondary education.

Describes the 'Pay It Forward model' as a system in which the Secretary, a state, or an institution of higher education (IHE) replaces students' need to borrow under the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program by covering all or part of their cost of attending an IHE in exchange for their agreement to contribute a certain percentage (not to exceed 5%) of their annual income for a specified number of years.

topic.name: Public Health Preparedness and Access
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures aimed at enhancing public health preparedness, access to healthcare services, and addressing the healthcare needs of specific populations.

Document 1: Strengthening National Preparedness and Response for Public Health Emergencies
monitor emerging issues and concerns as they relate to medical and public health preparedness
Document 2: raise the health status of Indians and Urban Indians
allow Indians to set their own health care priorities
Document 3: improve primary care education and training for medical students
establish an educational loan repayment program for individuals who agree to serve as primary care physicians

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Public Services
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at improving public services and support systems, including hospice care, emergency services, and resource management.

Document 1: Hospice Support Act of 2009
Medicare hospice budget neutrality adjustment factor
Document 2: American Soda Ash Competitiveness Act
royalty rate on the quantity or gross value of the output of sodium compounds
Document 3: Volunteer Firefighter Fairness Act of 2011
qualified emergency service volunteers as independent contractors

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments related to higher education, including financial assistance, educational outcomes, and eligibility criteria for institutions.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires institutions of higher education to inform prospective and enrolled students of educational assistance programs.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to include accelerated degree nursing students
Raises the yearly loan amounts available to all nursing students through that Program.
Document 3: amends title IV (Student Aid) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
makes an institution of higher education (IHE) that is a sanctuary campus ineligible for funds under title IV.

topic.name: Maritime Safety and Environmental Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety, security, and environmental protection in maritime operations.

Document 1: requires the Department of Transportation to report on the program for disposal of government-owned merchant vessels
amended to declare that nothing in the mandate for the EPA to regulate PCBs shall prohibit the dismantling of a vessel for recycling
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Energy to require the development and deployment of technology for oil spill response
doubles the limits on the total liability of the party responsible for a vessel from which oil is discharged
Document 3: establish an international committee for the safe handling of hazardous cargo
develop a strategic plan to utilize assistance programs for foreign ports in implementing antiterrorism measures

topic.name: Native American Rights and Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at recognizing and supporting the rights and needs of Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Approves the Fort Belknap Indian Community-Montana Water Rights Compact
Requires the tribal water rights to be held in trust by the United States for the Fort Belknap Indian Community
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe
Makes all U.S. laws of general applicability to Indians applicable to the Tribe
Document 3: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act
Reauthorizes appropriations for the block grants through FY2018

topic.name: Child and Family Welfare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the welfare and protection of children and families, focusing on adoption incentives and violence against women.

Document 1: Strengthening and Finding Families for Children Act - Subtitle A: Adoption Incentive Payments
Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act
Document 2: Violence Against Women Reauthorization Act of 2012 - Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994
Expands definitions of several terms for purposes of such Act
Document 3: Violence Against Women Reauthorization Act of 2013 - Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994
Modifies or expands grant conditions under such Act

topic.name: Education Policy and Advocacy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts to shape educational practices, protect student rights, and enhance safety within educational institutions.

Document 1: Expresses the sense of Congress that state and local prerogatives over elementary and secondary education need to be preserved.

Prohibits any federal officer or employee from directly or indirectly... conditioning the availability of financial support on a state's, LEA's, or school's adoption of any specific instructional content.
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require each institution of higher education... to designate an independent advocate for campus sexual assault prevention and response.
Directs each Advocate to ensure that sexual assault victims at the IHE are able to receive... specified information and services.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants and enter contracts to train elementary and secondary school nurses...
Requires this training to prepare school nurses to: (1) protect and preserve life; (2) notify public health authorities...

topic.name: Veterans and Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to veterans' access to healthcare services and the broader implications for Medicaid and Medicare programs.

Document 1: Veterans Access to Care Act
automatically designate medical facilities of the Department of Veterans Affairs as health professional shortage areas
Document 2: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
repeal the exclusion from medical assistance under the Medicaid program
Document 3: Medicare Patient Access to Hospice Act of 2015
recognize attending physician assistants as attending physicians to serve hospice patients

topic.name: Clean Energy and Emissions Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at promoting clean energy technologies and regulating greenhouse gas emissions in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes in the Treasury the Clean Energy Investment Fund
Defines 'clean energy technology' to mean a technology related to the production, use, transmission, storage, control, or conservation of energy
Document 2: Establishment of a federal greenhouse gas registry
Defines 'greenhouse gas' as carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, and other gases determined to contribute to climate change
Document 3: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
Defines 'GHG emissions' to mean emissions of a GHG, including stationary combustion source emissions

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation aimed at regulating and providing relief in financial markets, including credit unions and insurance related to terrorism and climate change.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to authorize the National Credit Union Administration Board (NCUAB) to delay regulation application to credit unions.
Prohibits a federal credit union from applying for permission to comply with state law if it would conflict with federal limitations on member business loans.
Document 2: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 (TRIA) to extend the Terrorism Insurance Program through December 31, 2020.
Specifies the aggregate industry insured losses resulting from certified acts of terror which will trigger the federal share of compensation under the Program.
Document 3: Establishes the Carbon Market Efficiency Board to analyze market information to promote understanding of the dynamics of each federal greenhouse gas (GHG) emission market.
Requires the Board to report to the President and Congress on the status of each federal GHG market, the economic effects of the markets.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at regulating environmental standards and promoting compliance in various sectors including chemical facilities and vehicle emissions.

Document 1: prohibiting the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) from applying regulations establishing risk-based performance standards for the security of chemical facilities
technical assistance regarding compliance with such regulations to any facility or works operator.
Document 2: calculate a carbon score for legislation
projected net greenhouse gas emissions that would result from enactment and implementation of a bill
Document 3: require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to waive requirements for recertification of kits
waive emission certification system requirements for a vehicle that is more than 10 years old or has more than 120,000 miles

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the evaluation and provision of benefits and services for military members and veterans, including disability evaluations, health registries, and credentialing opportunities.

Document 1: Restoring Disability Benefits for Injured and Wounded Warriors Act of 2007
requires the Secretary to report to Congress on the adequacy of the number of judge advocates assigned to physical evaluation board cases.
Document 2: establish and maintain a special record to be known as the Canadian Forces Base Gagetown Health Registry
notify individuals in the Registry of significant developments in research on the health consequences of potential exposure to a toxic substance.
Document 3: make information on civilian credentialing opportunities available to members of the Armed Forces
allows members or veterans to use educational assistance provided through the Department of Defense or the Department of Veterans Affairs in pursuit of a civilian certification or license.

topic.name: Campus and Forensic Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety and forensic science standards in educational institutions and federal research.

Document 1: Strengthening the safety and security of institutions of higher education (IHEs)
Developing protocols and best practices to prevent, protect against, and respond to dangerous and violent situations
Document 2: Establishes a National Forensic Science Research Initiative to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences
Establishes a National Forensic Science Coordinating Office to coordinate with relevant federal agencies
Document 3: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences
Requires the Director of the National Science Foundation (NSF) to enter into an arrangement with the National Academies

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the enhancement and provision of benefits for veterans, including insurance, housing, and compensation matters.

Document 1: Veterans' Benefits Enhancement Act of 2008
Title I: Insurance Matters - Allows administrative costs of the Service-Disabled Veterans' Insurance program to be paid through premiums.
Document 2: Veterans' Benefits Enhancement Act of 2009
Increases from $20,000 to $30,000 the maximum amount of supplemental insurance provided through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) for totally disabled veterans.
Document 3: Military Construction and Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2011
Appropriates funds for FY2011 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force.

topic.name: Government Policy and Support Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses various government initiatives aimed at providing support in agriculture, trade, and military service.

Document 1: Extends agricultural programs under the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act
Suspends permanent price support authorities
Document 2: Extends and modifies the temporary reduction of duty on monocarboxylic fatty acids
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers to designate income tax overpayment
Supports reservists and National Guard members through tax benefits

topic.name: Border Security and Immigration Reform
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at enhancing border security and reforming immigration policies in the United States.

Document 1: develop and submit to Congress a National Strategy for Border Security
establish a Southern Border Security Task Force
Document 2: Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for migration and refugee assistance
contributions for international peacekeeping activities
Document 3: establishes a Southern Border Security Commission
submit to Congress a comprehensive strategy for gaining situational awareness and operational control along the Southwest border

topic.name: Election and Gambling Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to election integrity, campaign financing, and the regulation of internet gambling.

Document 1: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
establishes the Clean Money Fund
Document 2: impose a tax on each Internet gambling licensee
require such licensees to keep daily records of gambling deposits
Document 3: prohibit any person from knowingly deceiving any other person regarding federal election
criminal penalty for deceptive acts

topic.name: Tax Legislation and Incentives
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax benefits and incentives for individuals and families.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2007
imposes a graduated increase in capital gains tax rates
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the allowance for payment of expenses for computer technology
allow a tax credit for contributions to such programs
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a medical care tax deduction for qualified sports and fitness expenses
Defines 'qualified sports and fitness expenses' as amounts paid for fitness facility memberships

topic.name: Legislative Commemorations and Acknowledgments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts that authorize commemorative works and acknowledgments of significant historical events or figures in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the World War I Memorial Foundation to establish a commemorative work
rededicating the District of Columbia War Memorial as a National and District of Columbia World War I Memorial.
Document 2: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
to make Public Company Accounting Oversight Board disciplinary proceedings public.
Document 3: Authorizes the Architect of the Capitol to permit the acknowledgment of God on flag certificates
issued at the request of a Member of the United States House of Representatives or of the United States Senate.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Taxation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions, interest rates, and tax policies to promote economic stability and growth.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit the annual percentage rate of interest (APR) applicable to any extension of credit to a consumer from exceeding 15%
Subjects a creditor who violates this Act to a civil penalty.
Document 2: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require an issuer that is a savings and loan holding company to register with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)

Suspends automatically the duty of a savings and loan holding company to file supplementary and periodic information if the securities of each class to which the registration statement relates are held of record by fewer than 1,200 persons.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to prescribe a 0% capital gains rate for individuals

Establishes in the Department of the Treasury a Distressed Assets Loan Fund under the control of the Secretary of the Treasury to make loans available at low rates of interest to depository institutions with distressed assets.

topic.name: Healthcare Legislation Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses amendments to the Social Security Act related to healthcare programs and services.

Document 1: Improving Access to Clinical Trials Act of 2009
disregard for SSI income eligibility purposes any compensation for participation in clinical trials
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
authorize direct payment under part B to a pharmacy for certain covered compound drugs
Document 3: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
annual audit of payment claims under a state Medicaid plan
